theme: Q2 Earnings Season Peak — FAANG + Big Tech Dense Reporting
methodology: news_first_then_verify: search pre-trade context → form thesis → verify with yfinance prices

data_sources.news: WebSearch: SEC 8-K filings, CNBC, Bloomberg, Shacknews, Fortune, Futurum, Alpha Spread
data_sources.earnings_facts: SEC EDGAR 8-K primary source cross-referenced with CNBC earnings reports

macro_context.period_summary: Peak Q2 earnings season with FAANG+MSFT all reporting within 10-day window. FOMC meeting July 29-30 (no rate change, Fed on hold). Market hovering near all-time highs. AI capex narrative dominant — GOOGL raised 2025 capex to $85B, MSFT/AMZN also heavy AI infra spenders. Tariff backdrop: AAPL flagged $800M tariff cost but India manufacturing offset narrative emerging.
macro_context.fomc_july_2025: FOMC meeting July 29-30. No rate change. Fed minutes showed median path of 2 cuts in H2 2025. Market pricing 1-2 cuts by year-end. Rates on hold = neutral-to-positive for growth stocks.

GOOGL.key_metrics: Cloud +32% to $13.62B; Search $54.19B; YouTube ads $9.80B; Net income +19% to $28.2B; Op margin 32.4%

TSLA.key_metrics: Revenue -12% YoY; Op income -42% YoY; Op margin 4.1%; Deliveries 384K vehicles; Second consecutive revenue decline

META.key_metrics: DAP 3.48B (+6% YoY); Costs $27.07B (+12% YoY); EPS beat by ~2.4%
META.source: Meta 8-K SEC EDGAR + PR Newswire Jul 30 2025

MSFT.key_metrics: Azure surpassed $75B ARR; AI revenue strong; Op income $34.3B +23%
MSFT.note: Stock opened gap-up Jul 31 to $550.83 then gave back gains to close $529.27 — buy-the-news sell-the-fact pattern

AMZN.key_metrics: Revenue +13% YoY; International profit +448%; AWS op income missed estimate
AMZN.stock_reaction: Initial AH +1.7%; Premarket Aug 1 -7.54%; Final Aug 1 close -8.3% (234.11 → 214.75)
AMZN.reason_for_drop: Guidance disappointed; AWS op income missed; heavy AI infra capex weighing on near-term margins
AMZN.source: AMZN 8-K SEC EDGAR + CNBC + Futurum Jul 31 2025

AAPL.india_manufacturing: 50% of US iPhones made in India that quarter
AAPL.key_metrics: June quarter revenue record; All geographies grew; AI integration ongoing
AAPL.stock_reaction_day1: Muted: closed Aug 1 at $201.58 vs Jul 31 $206.75 (-2.5%); tariff concern overhang
AAPL.post_earnings_drift: Massive upward drift: Aug 1 $201.58 → Aug 8 $228.44 (+13.4% in 6 trading days)
AAPL.source: AAPL 8-K SEC EDGAR + Apple Newsroom Jul 31 2025 + Fortune Aug 1 2025

trading_days.pre_trade_context: GOOGL and TSLA both report Q2 after market close today. Market at ATH vicinity (SPY ~628). GOOGL expectations: Cloud acceleration, AI monetization story intact, consensus bullish. TSLA expectations: deliveries miss pre-flagged, but bear case is -10%+ on macro weakness. Implied volatility elevated on both. AI capex narrative — MSFT/META/AMZN all reporting within 7 days.

trade_ideas.thesis: TSLA Q2 reporting tonight. Pre-flagged delivery miss (384K vs estimate ~390K). Revenue -12% YoY second consecutive decline. Op margin collapsed to 4.1%. No near-term catalyst — FSD robotaxi narrative priced in, fundamentals deteriorating. Short into earnings with stop above recent high ~340.
trade_ideas.risk: Musk robotaxi announcement or FSD surprise could gap up 15%+; high short interest ~10% creates squeeze risk

trade_ideas.instrument: Options — ATM strangle (buy $190 call + buy $189 put, 2-week expiry)
trade_ideas.thesis: GOOGL reports tonight. Cloud and Search both have potential positive surprise (AI-driven cloud growth, Search resilient vs fear). But stock already near 52-week high — any guidance miss or capex shock could cause -5% move. Strangle captures move in either direction. Break-even at ±3.7% from $189 strike.
trade_ideas.risk: Stock stays flat post earnings; both legs decay to near zero

trade_ideas.thesis: Pre-positioning ahead of META earnings on July 30. META has best-in-class ad revenue momentum, Llama AI cost advantage, capex is self-funded by cash flow. Track record of beats + guidance raises. Q2 should show continued ad acceleration. Risk/reward skewed long.
trade_ideas.risk: Regulatory headwinds (EU); ad market softening; AI capex spike exceeds guidance

TSLA_T07001.actual_outcome: TSLA closed 332.56 on Jul 23, opened 310.00 on Jul 24 — gap DOWN -6.8% on earnings miss. By Jul 24 close 305.30 = -8.2% from entry.
TSLA_T07001.verdict: WIN — thesis correct. Delivery miss + op margin collapse = sell-off as expected.

GOOGL_T07002.actual_outcome: GOOGL reported Q2 beat (EPS $2.31 vs $2.18, Rev $96.4B). Stock gapped UP: Jul 23 close 189.80 → Jul 24 open 196.59 (+3.6% gap). Call leg: profitable. Put leg: near-zero. Net strangle P&L: call worth ~$6.59 (intrinsic) vs total premium $7.00 — roughly breakeven to slight loss on strangle; directional call would have been better.
GOOGL_T07002.pnl_pct: -5% on strangle (call offset put loss; net slight loss after premium)
GOOGL_T07002.verdict: PARTIAL — strangle captured direction but gap-up didn't exceed break-even sufficiently; straight call would have been +47%.

META_T07003.actual_outcome: META entered at 711.91 on Jul 23. By Jul 30 (day before earnings) META had drifted to 693.58 (-2.6%). Then META reported blow-out Q2: stock rocketed from 693.58 close Jul 30 → 771.63 close Jul 31 (+11.2%). Exit at ~771 = +8.3% from entry.
META_T07003.verdict: WIN — thesis correct. Beat + guidance raise = massive gap-up.

trading_days.lesson: TSLA miss was fully telegraphed by delivery data (public) — high conviction short. GOOGL beat was expected but magnitude drove straight call > strangle. META pre-earnings long = correct but required holding through -2.6% drawdown first.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: Post-GOOGL/TSLA earnings. TSLA gaps down ~-8%. GOOGL gaps up +3.6% on Cloud beat but gives back gains. Market: SPY 629.08 (+0.03%). Sector rotation beginning — semis firm (NVDA 173.71). Next major catalysts: MSFT+META reporting July 30 (6 trading days away). Positioning window opens.

trade_ideas.thesis: TSLA short from yesterday hit target (-8%). Cover at open (310 area) or cover at close 305. Do NOT chase short further — this is a mean-reversion risk zone. Possible Musk/FSD catalyst could spike 15%. Book profit.

MSFT reports FY2025 Q4 on July 30 afterhours. Azure acceleration thesis intact — cloud competition with AWS/Google for AI workloads. Consensus expects ~$74B revenue, Azure ~+33%. Track record of beats on Azure. Stock at $506 is reasonable entry with 6-day runway to earnings. Call provides leverage with defined risk.
trade_ideas.risk: Azure deceleration; AI infra capex bloats costs; miss on guidance

trade_ideas.thesis: GOOGL's $85B capex raise + continued AI infrastructure buildout signals sustained GPU/accelerator demand. NVDA not reporting yet but benefits from every hyperscaler's capex guidance. Long NVDA as a read-through from GOOGL/MSFT/META AI capex acceleration.
trade_ideas.risk: Export restrictions on China sales; BlackwellGPU supply chain disruption; AI bubble narrative

TSLA_T07004.actual_outcome: TSLA continued slide from 310 open to 305.30 close on Jul 24. Covering at open ($310) was slightly early vs close; covering at close $305 = max profit. Closed short position.

MSFT_T07005.actual_outcome: MSFT entered $506.83 on Jul 24. On Jul 31 (day after earnings), MSFT gapped UP to $550.83 open (+8.7% from entry) then closed $529.27 (+4.4% from entry). $510 call would have been deep ITM. Massive winner.
MSFT_T07005.pnl_pct: +4.4% on stock; call estimated +200%+ depending on delta/premium paid
MSFT_T07005.verdict: WIN — Azure beat of 34% vs ~33% expected, massive positive reaction.

NVDA_T07006.actual_outcome: NVDA entered $173.71 on Jul 24. By Aug 5 NVDA was at $178.23 (+2.6%). Not a big mover in the short term but held above entry. Post-period: held steady around 178-183 range. Hyperscaler AI capex read-through was correct directionally.
NVDA_T07006.verdict: WIN (modest) — correct thesis but NVDA needed more time to fully react to capex upcycle.

trading_days.lesson: Post-earnings day: cover shorts promptly, don't get greedy. The MSFT pre-earnings call setup (6 days runway + Azure beat thesis) was the highest conviction trade of the period. NVDA as capex read-through works but is slow.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: Quiet day before FOMC week + mega-tech earnings. SPY +0.3% to 631.74. Market grinding higher. TSLA stabilizing. Pre-FOMC positioning. META/MSFT earnings in 3 trading days. Semi sector firming. Key question: will FOMC signal cuts? Consensus: hold, dovish tone.

trade_ideas.instrument: Options — Buy $232 straddle (ATM call + put, Aug 8 expiry)
trade_ideas.thesis: AMZN reports Q2 on July 31 afterhours. Massive earnings volatility historically (avg ±8%). AWS +17% expected, but concern about AI capex drag on margins. Revenue beat likely ($167B+ vs $162B expected). Guidance/margin story uncertain. Buy straddle captures big move either direction. Historical implied move ~7-9% on AMZN earnings.
trade_ideas.risk: Implied vol crush post-earnings if move is small; premium loss if stock stays flat

trade_ideas.thesis: Pre-positioning for earnings week tailwind. 5 of 7 Mag7 reporting this week. If MSFT+META beat (high probability given GOOGL cloud beat signal), QQQ should run +2-3%. Risk defined by FOMC — any hawkish surprise would be negative but low probability.

AMZN_T07007.actual_outcome: AMZN closed $231.44 on Jul 25. After earnings Jul 31 (close $234.11), stock crashed on Aug 1 open to $217 area, close $214.75 = -8.3% from Jul 31 close. Move from Jul 25 entry: 231.44 → 214.75 = -7.2%. The PUT leg won massively. Straddle total: put +$16.69 intrinsic value, call near zero. Net gain ~$7-9 after premium.
AMZN_T07007.verdict: WIN — AWS op income miss + guidance disappointment drove the downside move. Straddle correctly captured big move.

QQQ_T07008.actual_outcome: QQQ entered $564.27 on Jul 25. By Aug 1 QQQ was $551.83 = -2.2%. The FOMC week was positive (hold + dovish signal) but AMZN collapse on Aug 1 dragged QQQ down. Exit at Aug 1 = loss.
QQQ_T07008.verdict: LOSS — QQQ thesis partially right (MSFT+META beat) but AMZN + AAPL day1 reaction dragged the index. Wrong to hold through Aug 1.

trading_days.lesson: Straddle on AMZN was right — high vol event, uncertain direction. QQQ broad long is lazy when individual names diverge wildly (META +11%, AMZN -8% same period). Stock-specific options > index ETF during earnings week.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: FOMC meeting starts today (July 29-30). Earnings week: MSFT + META both reporting Wednesday July 30 AH. Market quiet: SPY 631.58 (-0.03%). Semi sector recovering — NVDA 176.72 (+1.9%). Key pre-trade question: sell META into earnings or hold? Macro neutral. Big event risk this week. VIX likely elevated.

trade_ideas.thesis: META long from $711.91 holding. Now $715.95 (+0.6%). 2 days to earnings. GOOGL's strong ad-adjacent data (Search resilient, YouTube +) is a positive read-through for META's ad revenue. Add to position. Risk: if meta misses, -8 to -10% possible. Upside if beat: +10-15% (as seen in prior META earnings beats).
trade_ideas.risk: Regulatory fine / EU action; AI capex guidance shock

trade_ideas.thesis: NVDA moving on AI capex read-throughs from GOOGL ($85B), MSFT (heavy Azure AI), and META (heavy AI infra). All 3 are major NVDA GPU buyers. Pre-earnings positioning adding to NVDA ahead of hyperscaler confirmation of GPU demand. Not yet reporting — Blackwell ramp narrative intact.
trade_ideas.risk: Export controls tightened; AI inference doesn't require as many GPUs as training

META_T07009.actual_outcome: META added at $715.95. By Jul 31 close $771.63 = +7.8% on the add. Full position (T07-003 entry $711.91 + T07-009 add $715.95) averaged ~$714. Exit Jul 31 $771.63 = +8.0% blended.
META_T07009.verdict: WIN — correct to add pre-earnings given strong ad read-through from GOOGL.

NVDA_T07010.actual_outcome: NVDA added $176.72. By Aug 5 $178.23 = +0.9% in period. Slow mover. By Aug 8 $182.67 = +3.4%.
NVDA_T07010.verdict: WIN (modest) — directionally correct but slow to react.

trading_days.lesson: Adding to META 2 days before earnings when thesis is intact and read-throughs positive is high-conviction. The GOOGL ad beat was a direct signal for META. NVDA as infrastructure play is slow but directionally correct.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: FOMC Day 1. Market subdued — waiting on Fed. SPY 629.92 (-0.3%). TSLA showing recovery from earnings selloff (325.59 +2.6%). AMZN drift lower 231.01 (-0.8% — pre-earnings nervousness on margin fears). META/MSFT reporting tomorrow. Market very event-driven.
trading_days.notable: META sold off -2.8% intraday despite no news — pre-earnings profit-taking. Created better entry opportunity.

trade_ideas.thesis: META dips -2.8% with no negative news — pure pre-earnings profit-taking or rotation. Thesis unchanged: GOOGL ad beat = META ad tailwind. This dip is a gift. Add or hold firm. One more day to earnings.
trade_ideas.risk: Earnings miss sends to $640; hold with stop at $675

trade_ideas.thesis: AAPL reports Q3 FY2025 on July 31 AH alongside AMZN. iPhone +13% YoY expected; Services record. Tariff impact limited by India manufacturing ramp (50% of US iPhones now from India). $800M tariff cost manageable vs $94B revenue. Stock has been weak pre-earnings ($213→$210). Upside on beat + India narrative = potential +5-8% gap-up.
trade_ideas.risk: Guidance cautious on tariffs; AI integration disappointment; stock already priced for perfection in some metrics

META_T07011.actual_outcome: META dip buy at $698.36 on Jul 29. Next day Jul 30 before earnings: $693.58 (-0.7%). Then after earnings July 31: $771.63 (+11.2%). From $698.36 entry: +10.5%.
META_T07011.verdict: WIN — the pre-earnings dip was noise. Strong conviction justified holding and adding.

AAPL_T07012.actual_outcome: AAPL call entered with stock at $210.44. After earnings Jul 31 AH, stock DROPPED Aug 1 to $201.58 (-4.2% from entry). Muted reaction despite beat — tariff overhang sentiment. Aug 15 call taken in the $215 strike would have been OTM and decaying. HOWEVER: massive post-earnings drift: Aug 6 $212.41, Aug 7 $219.16, Aug 8 $228.44. If held to Aug 8 = +8.5% on stock. Aug 15 call would have recovered strongly.
AAPL_T07012.verdict: MIXED — initial reaction bearish (tariff fear dominated), but India manufacturing + record results drove massive post-earnings drift. Aug 15 expiry call was the right vehicle — gave time to recover.

trading_days.lesson: Pre-earnings dips in mega-cap tech with intact thesis are buying opportunities (META). AAPL post-earnings behavior was unusual: beat + miss in sentiment simultaneously. Tariff fear > earnings beat on day 1, but India supply chain pivot was a powerful multi-day catalyst.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: FOMC Day 2 — decision released 2pm ET. No rate change expected. META + MSFT both reporting after close TODAY. Market quiet: SPY -0.2% to 629.12. META at 693.58 (-0.7%). This is the biggest single day of the earnings season — two $1T+ market cap companies reporting simultaneously. High vol expected.
trading_days.fomc_result: Fed held rates unchanged. Dovish tone — acknowledged inflation progress. Market barely moved.

trade_ideas.direction: HOLD ALL LONG / SELL PART AH IF GAP-UP
trade_ideas.thesis: Full META position (T07-003, T07-009, T07-011) held into earnings. Blended avg ~$708. Plan: if stock gaps +8%+ on results → trim 50% at open, hold rest for drift. If gaps down on miss → cut at open.
MSFT calls from Jul 24 ($506.83 entry on stock, $510 call). Azure beat consensus — $75B+ ARR milestone, AI revenue surging. Expect 5-10% gap-up. Plan: sell calls at open July 31.
AAPL reports tonight alongside AMZN. Beat expected on iPhone+Services. India manufacturing = tariff shield narrative. Hold call through earnings.

META_T07013.actual_outcome: META closed $693.58 Jul 30. After earnings: Beat ($47.52B revenue +22%, Q3 guide $47.5-50.5B). July 31: OPEN $773.38, CLOSE $771.63 = +11.2% from $693.58. Trim 50% at open $773 = excellent. Hold rest caught the close.
META_T07013.verdict: WIN — massive beat + guidance raise. The largest single-day winner of the backtest period.

MSFT_T07014.actual_outcome: MSFT closed $509.17 Jul 30. After earnings (Azure +34%, Rev $76.4B): Jul 31 OPEN $550.83 (+8.2%) then pulled back to $529.27 close (+3.9%). $510 call at open was worth ~$40+ intrinsic vs probably ~$3-4 premium paid. Massive return. Sold at open was optimal.
MSFT_T07014.pnl_pct: +200%+ on the call option; +8.2% on stock equivalent at open
MSFT_T07014.verdict: WIN — Azure beat + AI momentum drove the spike.

trading_days.lesson: Holding into earnings on high-conviction positions (META, MSFT) with clear catalyst = massive payoff. The key was having done the work beforehand (GOOGL cloud beat as read-through for MSFT Azure). Trim on the gap-up open is smart risk management — don't be greedy holding 100%.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: Post-META/MSFT earnings day. META gapped +11.2%, MSFT +3.9% on open. Market digesting: QQQ up strongly. AMZN + AAPL both reporting AH TODAY. Massive vol event: two more Mag7 reports. AMZN margin risk (AWS op income concerns known pre-earnings). AAPL: tariff overhang vs India manufacturing. Net position: META/MSFT profits locked. Setting up for AMZN/AAPL.
trading_days.notable: Market sold off late in day despite Meta/MSFT beats — AMZN concern weighing, profit-taking from earnings winners. SPY -1.2% from open.

trade_ideas.thesis: Locked massive gains. Post-earnings drift possible (META had strong drift after prior beats) but risk/reward less compelling at $771. Trim half, hold rest with trailing stop.

AMZN straddle from Jul 25 at $231.44. Reporting tonight. AWS op income concern (estimates $10.9B, company may print $10.2B). Revenue beat likely. Margin guidance the key variable. If guidance disappoints → put leg wins big. Hold straddle through tonight.

AAPL reporting tonight. India manufacturing story is a genuine catalyst (50% of US iPhones from India = tariff relief). Services record expected. Beat likely. However stock already ran from $206 in recent weeks. Risk: muted reaction if priced in.

AMZN_T07017.actual_outcome: AMZN at $234.11 close Jul 31. After earnings: massive beat on revenue ($167.7B vs $162B, EPS $1.68 vs $1.32 +27%). BUT AWS op income $10.2B missed $10.9B estimate. Guidance disappointed. Stock: Aug 1 OPEN ~$217, CLOSE $214.75 = -8.3% from Jul 31 close. PUT leg of straddle worth ~$19+ intrinsic. CALL leg worthless. Net straddle P&L from Jul 25 entry ($231.44): put strike $232 → realized $232-$214.75 = $17.25 intrinsic on put. Total straddle premium paid ~$14-16. Net gain ~$1-3 per share or roughly breakeven to +20%.
AMZN_T07017.verdict: WIN — put leg carried. Thesis about AMZN directional uncertainty was correct; margin miss was the driver.

AAPL_T07018.actual_outcome: AAPL reported Q3 FY2025 beat (Rev $94B +10%, EPS $1.57 +12%, iPhone +13%). BUT Aug 1 reaction: stock dropped to $201.58 (-2.5% from $206.75). Tariff concern + muted AI narrative dominated. $215 call OTM and losing value day 1. However by Aug 6-8: AAPL surged to $212.41, $219.16, $228.44 = +10.4% from Aug 1 low. Aug 15 call recovered massively.
AAPL_T07018.pnl_pct: -20% day1 on call; +60%+ by Aug 8 if held
AAPL_T07018.verdict: HOLD — Initial pain but fundamental drift proved correct. Key lesson: AAPL had the MOST significant post-earnings drift of the period (+13.4% from Aug 1 to Aug 8).

trading_days.lesson: AMZN: revenue beats can coexist with margin misses, and guidance is what matters for the stock. The AWS op income miss + cautious guidance = -8% despite revenue beat. AAPL: muted day-1 reaction ≠ wrong thesis. The India manufacturing pivot + Services record + tariff relief was a slow-burn catalyst that took 6 trading days to fully price.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: Major event risk day: AMZN crashes -8.3% at open. AAPL down -2.5%. SPY -1.2% to $616.49. Market giveback after mega-tech earnings week. QQQ -2.4%. Sentiment: profit-taking + AMZN alarm. Key question: is AMZN drop a buying opportunity or does it signal margin pressure across cloud? AAPL weakness: tariff fear or real? Volume on AAPL: 104M shares (double normal) = institutional selling.

trade_ideas.thesis: Straddle PUT leg wins big today. Close straddle at open — lock in gains. Evaluate whether AMZN at $214-217 is a buy: AWS is structurally growing (+17%), EPS beat by 27%, drop is margin guidance fear not fundamental deterioration. However August = more uncertainty. Don't rush to buy the dip today.

AAPL at $201.58 after reporting $94B revenue beat, iPhone +13%, Services record. The -2.5% reaction is tariff fear not fundamental deterioration. India manufacturing pivot (50% of US iPhones from India) directly addresses tariff risk. This is a gift entry. Add stock long to complement the $215 call already held. India narrative = multi-week tailwind.
trade_ideas.risk: Tariff escalation with India specifically; Apple Intelligence disappointment; China demand deterioration

trade_ideas.thesis: Market down -1.2% today. Risk-off from AMZN guidance miss. FOMC just concluded. August historically weak seasonality. Multiple Mag7 already reported — no more near-term earnings catalysts for index. Buy puts as portfolio hedge for next 1 week.
trade_ideas.risk: Market bounces on positive macro; puts decay if SPY holds

AMZN_T07019.actual_outcome: Straddle closed at open — put leg intrinsic ~$17. Booked. Decision not to buy AMZN dip immediately: AMZN held $214-217 range for 3 days (Aug 1-5), then bounced to $222 by Aug 6. Missing the bottom by not buying Aug 1.
AMZN_T07019.verdict: CORRECT to close straddle. NEUTRAL on no immediate buy — AMZN did stabilize but gave limited upside in next 5 days.

AAPL_T07020.actual_outcome: AAPL bought at $201.58. Price action: Aug 4 $202.55, Aug 5 $202.12, Aug 6 $212.41 (+5.4%), Aug 7 $219.16, Aug 8 $228.44 (+13.4% from entry in 6 days). MASSIVE win. India manufacturing + fundamental quality drove the drift.
AAPL_T07020.verdict: STAR TRADE of the period — buying AAPL on the post-earnings dip when the thesis was intact = highest return trade.

SPY_T07021.actual_outcome: SPY: Aug 1 $616.49, Aug 4 $625.86 (+1.5%), Aug 5 $622.69, Aug 6 $627.46. Market bounced back. $615 put decayed. Loss on hedge.
SPY_T07021.verdict: LOSS — market bounced faster than expected. Hedge was expensive.

trading_days.lesson: Post-earnings dip buying of quality companies (AAPL with intact thesis, India narrative, fundamental beat) is the highest-alpha trade in earnings season. The SPY hedge was wrong — individual stock work > index macro call. When the thesis explains why the stock is down (tariff fear) vs is the fundamental broken (no), buy the dip.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: Post-earnings-week market reset. SPY 625.86 (+1.5% bounce). AAPL recovering from $201.58 base. AMZN stabilizing. META/MSFT pulling back from gap-up levels (META $774→$748). Semi sector: NVDA $179.97 (+3.6% today). Market entering calmer period — next major catalyst is NVDA earnings (late Aug). TSLA showed recovery to $309.

trade_ideas.thesis: AAPL position holding. Small gain so far (+0.5%) but India manufacturing narrative building. Multi-week drift thesis intact. No rush to sell — target $220+.

NVDA breaking out on hyperscaler AI capex confirmation (GOOGL $85B, MSFT heavy, META heavy). All three are confirmed NVDA customers. Blackwell ramp narrative + supply tight. Next catalyst: NVDA own earnings in late August. Add to existing position.
trade_ideas.risk: Export controls; China restrictions; Blackwell supply issues

AAPL_T07022.actual_outcome: AAPL: Aug 4 $202.55, Aug 5 $202.12, Aug 6 $212.41 (+4.9%), Aug 7 $219.16, Aug 8 $228.44. Holding was exactly right.
AAPL_T07022.verdict: WIN — patience through the base period (Aug 1-5) was rewarded with explosive move Aug 6-8.

NVDA_T07023.verdict: WIN (modest) — correct thesis, needs more time.

trading_days.lesson: After earnings season, the market enters a quieter period. Best remaining trades: post-earnings drift plays (AAPL) and pre-next-catalyst accumulation (NVDA). Individual stock selection > broad macro bets.

trading_days.pre_trade_context: SPY 622.69 (-0.5%). AAPL $202.12 (+0.3% from entry, still building base). AMZN $213.75. NVDA $178.23. Market in post-earnings digestion. No major macro catalysts. AAPL India narrative getting media coverage (Benzinga, Fortune articles). Set up for AAPL acceleration. Ending official backtest period.

trade_ideas.thesis: AAPL base at $201-203 over 4 trading days since earnings. Every day that passes without selling confirms the support. India manufacturing = structural positive. Next leg up when narrative penetrates broader market. Aug 15 call still alive and OTM but theta burning — need move by Aug 12.
trade_ideas.forward_view: AAPL will surge Aug 6-8 on India narrative + fundamental quality recognition

AAPL_T07024.actual_outcome: AAPL at $202.12 on Aug 5. Then: Aug 6 $212.41 (+5.1%), Aug 7 $219.16 (+3.2%), Aug 8 $228.44 (+4.2%). Total from Aug 5: +13.0% in 3 days. $215 call went from OTM to deep ITM.
AAPL_T07024.verdict: STAR — the base building made the eventual breakout high-conviction.

trading_days.lesson: Post-earnings base building is a classic pattern. AAPL spent Aug 1-5 forming a tight base at $201-203, then broke out violently Aug 6-8. The $215 call was the right vehicle: defined risk through the uncertain period, massive leverage on the breakout.

top_trades.description: Post-earnings dip buy at $201.58, India manufacturing thesis, held to Aug 8 $228.44
Pre-earnings long + dip buy, held through beat, trimmed at gap-up
Pre-earnings call setup, Azure beat delivered +200%+ on options
Short into earnings miss, covered next day
Earnings straddle on uncertain margin story, put leg won

worst_trades.description: Market bounced fast, hedge expired worthless
Index long during earnings week with divergent single names
Strangle instead of straight call; move wasn't big enough for both legs

key_lessons.title: Earnings Read-Throughs Are Actionable Signals
key_lessons.detail: GOOGL Cloud beat (+32%) was a clear signal for MSFT Azure acceleration. Using this to buy MSFT calls 6 days before earnings was the highest risk/reward trade. Don't wait for confirmation — read-throughs decay in value the longer you wait.

key_lessons.title: Post-Earnings Drift: Day-1 Reaction ≠ Final Story
key_lessons.detail: AAPL dropped -2.5% on earnings beat (Aug 1) due to tariff fear, then rallied +13.4% over next 6 days as India manufacturing narrative penetrated. The day-1 muted/negative reaction on a fundamental beat is a buy signal if you understand WHY it's down and the reason is temporary sentiment not structural.

key_lessons.title: Straddle vs Straight Call: Know When Each is Right
key_lessons.detail: GOOGL: high probability beat in specific direction (Cloud strong, Search resilient) → straight call was better than strangle. AMZN: genuinely uncertain (revenue beat likely but margin direction unclear) → straddle was correct. Match the instrument to the directional confidence.

key_lessons.title: Revenue Beats Can Coexist with Stock Drops
key_lessons.detail: AMZN reported EPS +27% beat, revenue +3.5% beat, but dropped -8.3% because (a) AWS operating income missed by $700M vs estimate and (b) guidance was cautious. For cloud/platform companies, margin and guidance dominate over revenue beats. Always model AWS/Azure/Cloud margin explicitly.

key_lessons.title: Pre-Earnings Dips in High-Conviction Names = Entries
key_lessons.detail: META dipped -2.8% on July 29 with no negative news — pure profit-taking. This was a gift entry 1 day before earnings. The dip to $698 vs eventual close $771 = 10.5% gain on the dip buy. Pre-earnings dips in names with intact thesis and positive read-throughs are rare high-conviction entries.

key_lessons.title: Index ETF Long is Lazy During Single-Stock Earnings Week
key_lessons.detail: QQQ long underperformed because META +11% and AMZN -8% partially offset each other in the index. Individual stock options captured the full divergence. During concentrated earnings week, stock-specific options > index ETF.

key_lessons.title: Publicly Available Data Can Drive High-Conviction Shorts
key_lessons.detail: TSLA delivery data was public before earnings — 384K deliveries vs ~390K expected, and the -12% revenue YoY trajectory was clear. This was a >HIGH conviction short. Using public operational data (deliveries, production, occupancy, etc.) to pre-identify earnings misses is a repeatable edge.

key_lessons.title: MSFT Azure Gap-Up: Buy at Open or Wait for Pullback
key_lessons.detail: MSFT gapped up to $550.83 open (+8.2%) then pulled back to $529.27 by close (-3.7% from open). Selling the call at the open was optimal. Never hold into a gap-up through the full day on earnings if you have 100%+ gain — the first 30 minutes are the best exit window.

meta_observations.earnings_season_pattern: Q2 2025 peak earnings season showed: (1) cloud/AI names outperformed on capex narrative (MSFT, GOOGL, META), (2) consumer hardware faced tariff headwinds (AAPL day-1 muted) but resolved on supply chain pivots, (3) the 'guidance > revenue beat' principle held for AMZN, (4) TSLA fundamentals deteriorating = short worked cleanly.
meta_observations.ai_capex_theme: Every major tech company raised or maintained AI infrastructure spending. GOOGL $85B, MSFT heavy Azure AI, META heavy compute. This was bullish for NVDA (infrastructure layer) and bearish for near-term margins (costs rising). The capex arms race created a bifurcated market: GPU/infra beneficiaries vs software companies absorbing costs.
meta_observations.india_manufacturing_aapl: AAPL's pivot to India for US iPhone production (50% by Q3 FY2025) was the single most important supply chain story of the period. It directly addressed the $800M tariff cost and the broader tariff risk overhang. The market took 6 trading days to fully price this in — a classic information diffusion lag.
